facts.shortSummary: The party smashes through the locked gate, follows the hidden tunnel under Raven's Hold, and regroups in a disused storeroom once Wellby returns.
facts.longSummary: Unable to pick the rusted lock, the party has Riswynn break the gate and then pushes deeper through the hidden tunnel toward Raven's Hold. The passage twists up into an old storeroom, where they haul everyone inside by rope and rejoin Wellby after his separate look around the dwarven outpost.

location.context: the hidden tunnel, rope shaft, and first abandoned storeroom beneath the hold
location.notes: The beat covers the party's entry from the underground route into the old interior chambers.

facts.shortSummary: In the storeroom, the party compares what it knows about Raven's Hold, the valley, and the iron guardian while arguing over whether to retreat or keep scouting.
facts.longSummary: Safe for a moment in the abandoned storeroom, the party trades reports about the ruined outpost, the hidden tunnel, the hyena-like raiders, and the large animated iron figure guarding the dwarven side of the valley. The discussion stretches into maps, theories, and worries about splitting up before they decide they still need better information from inside Raven's Hold itself.

location.context: the abandoned storeroom beneath the hold where the party pauses to plan
location.notes: The party remains in the same safe chamber while debating the next move.

npcs.name: animated iron guardian
npcs.notes: Wellby reports seeing the great iron figure apparently standing watch over part of the old outpost.

facts.shortSummary: Delwath scouts ahead invisibly, finding undead hobgoblins in an old prison and identifying both the northern entrance approach and the southern tower access.
facts.longSummary: To break the deadlock, Delwath creeps ahead under invisibility and discovers that the nearby caverns once served as a prison. There he finds two undead hobgoblins rummaging among skeletons, hears the pack noise of animals and hyenas near the northern approach, and confirms that a southern passage leads across a small bridge to the towers overlooking the inner stronghold.

location.context: the prison caverns and approach passages beneath the hold
location.notes: The beat shifts from the storeroom into the larger under-keep chambers that connect to the entrance and tower routes.

npcs.name: undead hobgoblins
npcs.notes: Delwath finds two undead hobgoblins snapping bones among the prison dead.

npcs.name: gnolls
npcs.context: guards near the entrance side of the hold
npcs.notes: The northern approach suggests living guards and penned animals remain active closer to the surface.

facts.shortSummary: The party ambushes the two undead hobgoblins in the prison chamber and then confirms the room was once used for imprisonment and execution.
facts.longSummary: Once Delwath reports back, the party decides the undead hobgoblins are too dangerous to leave roaming behind them and stages a quick ambush in the prison room. After destroying the creatures, they search the chamber and identify iron manacles, decapitated skeletons, and other signs that the space once served as a hobgoblin prison and execution site.

location.context: the old prison and execution chamber beneath the hold
location.notes: The entire beat remains centered on the prison room where the undead were found.

npcs.name: undead hobgoblins
npcs.notes: The party destroys both undead hobgoblins before they can alert the rest of the hold.

mainEnemies.name: undead hobgoblins
mainEnemies.notes: Two undead guards haunting the prison chamber.

combat.notes: The fight is brief and contained entirely within the old prison room.

facts.shortSummary: From the prison exits, the party studies Raven's Hold, sees gnolls and animal pens near the entrance, and decides to wait for darker conditions before sending Kenzo to scout the towers.
facts.longSummary: With the prison room secure, the party splits its attention between the northern overlook and the southern tower approach. The northern view reveals gnolls rotating watch at the entrance plaza and a noisy collection of caged or penned animals, while the southern view confirms the towers, gatehouse, and inner keep are all still in use. After comparing what each vantage point shows, the party settles on an invisible tower scout as the best next step once evening deepens.

location.context: the southern tower bridge approach and the northern rubble overlook near the entrance plaza
location.notes: The beat covers observation posts on both sides of the under-keep chambers.

npcs.name: gnolls
npcs.notes: The party watches gnolls posted at the entrance plaza and sees them rotate in groups.

facts.shortSummary: Kenzo enters the first tower invisibly, confirms it is mostly abandoned, and discovers signs that a Blackened Claw commander once used the upper rooms.
facts.longSummary: Once darkness gives them slightly better cover, Delwath turns Kenzo invisible and sends him across the bridge into the first tower. Kenzo finds empty lower levels, views into the courtyard where more undead hobgoblins roam, and a commander-style upper room marked with the painted symbol of the Blackened Claw, confirming that the stronghold was once used by that clan.

location.context: the first tower and gatehouse approach on the inner side of the hidden tunnel route
location.notes: Kenzo remains alone in the first tower throughout this reconnaissance pass.

npcs.name: undead hobgoblins
npcs.notes: Kenzo spots several more undead hobgoblins moving in the courtyard below.

organizations.name: Blackened Claw
organizations.notes: Kenzo finds the Blackened Claw mark painted on the wall of the old commander's chamber.

facts.shortSummary: Crossing into the second tower, Kenzo finds an orc, two suspicious guards, and a badly wounded prisoner who identifies herself as Vola.
facts.longSummary: Kenzo presses on from the first tower into the second and quickly realizes it is actively occupied. An orc has claimed one level for living quarters, two human-seeming guards prowl the upper floor, and in one cramped chamber Kenzo finds a badly wounded armored prisoner tied up on a bed of straw. He calms her, learns her name is Vola, and then slips back to bring the rest of the party in for a rescue.

location.context: the occupied second tower and its upper prison rooms
location.notes: The beat covers Kenzo's solo reconnaissance and discovery of the prisoner.

npcs.name: Vola
npcs.notes: Kenzo finds Vola bound and badly injured on the upper floor of the second tower.

npcs.name: orc
npcs.notes: An orc is using the third floor of the second tower as living quarters.

npcs.name: jackalwere guards
npcs.notes: Two apparently human guards patrol the upper level where Vola is being held.

facts.shortSummary: The rescue turns violent when the second tower guards react, and the party defeats the orc and the two jackalweres before freeing Vola.
facts.longSummary: After Kenzo guides the others into position, the group tries to seize the tower before the captors can react, but the confrontation quickly erupts into a close-quarters fight on the upper floors. One guard drops asleep, the orc charges in, the other guard reveals a jackal-like hybrid form, and the party has to cut through all three enemies before the tower is safe enough to finish freeing Vola and gather her gear.

location.context: the upper floors of the occupied second tower
location.notes: The combat spans the stair landings and prison level of the second tower.

npcs.name: Vola
npcs.notes: Vola survives the fight and is freed once the tower is secured.

npcs.name: orc
npcs.notes: The orc joins the fight from the occupied floor below the prison room.

npcs.name: jackalwere guards
npcs.notes: The two guards reveal their jackalwere nature once combat begins.

mainEnemies.name: orc
mainEnemies.notes: The orc is the heaviest melee threat in the tower fight.

mainEnemies.name: jackalwere guards
mainEnemies.notes: Two shapechanging guards protect the prison floor and resist nonmagical weapons.

combat.notes: The rescue fight covers the full engagement in the second tower before the prisoner is recovered.

facts.shortSummary: Back in the safer tower, Vola explains that she serves the Society of the Open Scroll and warns the party that Agata Dustmother is hunting old imperial relics in Raven's Hold.
facts.longSummary: Once the fight is over, the party relocates to the quieter tower rooms, returns Vola's weapons, and questions her about why she was imprisoned here. Vola reveals that she is Chardonian and part of the Society of the Open Scroll, that Arcas was another member, and that her captors serve a dangerous fey power named Agata Dustmother who is searching Dunmar and the blasted plains for relics of lost empire. By the time the party finishes comparing what each side knows, it is about seven in the evening and they agree they still need to learn more before trying to escape.

location.context: the abandoned first tower where the party withdraws to question Vola after the rescue
location.notes: The conversation happens in the safer tower rooms after the captors in the second tower are dealt with.

npcs.name: Vola
npcs.notes: Vola identifies herself, recovers her gear, and explains why Agata's servants took her captive.

npcs.name: Agata Dustmother
npcs.notes: Vola says Agata and her servants are gathering imperial relics for some harmful purpose.

npcs.name: Arcas
npcs.notes: Vola confirms that Arcas was also associated with the Society of the Open Scroll.

items.name: imperial relics
items.notes: Vola says both her group and Agata's servants are searching the frontier for old relics left from imperial expansion and from Drankor's ruins.

organizations.name: Society of the Open Scroll
organizations.notes: Vola identifies herself and Arcas as members of this relic-hunting Chardonian society.

organizations.name: Great Library
organizations.notes: Vola connects the Society of the Open Scroll to hidden circles associated with the Great Library in Chardon.
